text: Hi everyone, my name is Courtney and today I'm going to talk about my experience playing basketball.
Basketball was one of my favorite sports growing up. I played basketball starting in middle school.
I was on several different basketball teams throughout middle school and high school.
My experience playing basketball was a bit of a roller coaster. My team was never very good.
My basketball team lost a lot of games, but I had a lot of fun being a part of the team.
In middle school, I remember that there was a one game where I was dribbling down the court.
I passed the ball to my teammate and the buzzer was about to end the game. It was almost the end of the game.
The crowd was chanting five, four, three, two, one. Right before the game ended, my teammate shot the ball.
The ball went into the net. She made the basket. The crowd cheered. Woo! My teammate had tied the game.
The score was tied. Both teams had the same amount of points. Now the game went into overtime.
Overtime is an extra period of play. If a game ties, then the players go into overtime.
I think overtime lasts an additional five minutes. During overtime, my team scored again.
Now, my team had one more point than the other team. The other team scored again.
And the score was tied. My teammate was dribbling the ball down the court. One of the other players hit my teammate.
The referee called a foul. You're not allowed to hit people in basketball.
My teammate got to shoot a free throw. A free throw is when a player fowls another player and they get to shoot some free baskets to make up for this violation of the rules.
My teammate stood at the free throw line. My teammate shot the ball once. She made it. My teammate made the basket.
Now the score was one point in our favor. On the second shot, my teammate shot again.
And she made it again. Now we were two points ahead. It was the other team's turn to have control of the ball.
They were on offense. My team was on defense. We all stood with our hands up to try and defend the basketball court.
We all stood defending our side of the court. The crowd was counting down again.
Five, four, three, two, the girl from the other team shot the ball from the half court line.
She shot the ball. It hit the backboard. It hit the backboard and fell to the left side of the basketball hoop.
That was close. The ball bounced off the backboard and a girl from the other team tried to catch it.
She tried to rebound the ball. But it was too late. The game was over and my team had won.
My team was not very good at basketball. So this was a big celebration for us. We were all so happy
that we won the basketball game. I had a similar experience in high school with my basketball team,
not being very good. We didn't play very well. I was generally in the position of point guard or
one of the wings. I never really played posts. Posts are the positions near the basket in the paint.
But I never really played post because post players are tall. I'm not that tall. So I always played
point guard, which is the person that brings the ball down the court or a wing. I loved playing basketball.
Unfortunately, my senior year of high school, I was cut from the basketball team.
We got a new coach and the new coach didn't think that my skills were good enough to play on the team.
I was sad, but it turned out that in the end it was okay. I found other activities to participate in.
I also tore my ACL playing basketball. The ACL is a ligament in your knee. I jumped up to grab the ball
and when I landed, my knee twisted. I heard a pop and my knee was very unstable and wobbly.
This is why the coach didn't want me to play basketball. I was recovering from this injury.
And like I said before, I wasn't a phenomenal player or very tall. Overall, my experience with basketball
was a roller coaster. But I'm really glad that I decided to be a part of the basketball team.
It taught me many lessons about what it feels like to lose, what it feels like to win,
and what it feels like to be a part of a team. Did you play basketball? Let me know some of your
favorite memories about playing basketball or about watching basketball in the comments.
I'm Courtney. Thanks for watching. See you later. Bye!
